projects.name: Estimation for Refund Implementaiton in Billing

user_stories.goal: I want to submit a refund request to the Billing system via a secure API
user_stories.benefit: so that the refund process can be initiated automatically from the originating application
user_stories.raw_text: We need to implement refund functionality in Billing. This will have impact in billing core to create credit and debit note, Integrations with 5 external systems out of which 3 are payment channels, 1 is upstream application from where the request is coming and last one is to send the refund information to ERP

user_stories.goal: I want to automatically generate a credit note upon receiving and validating a refund request
user_stories.benefit: so that the customer's account balance is correctly adjusted and documented in the core billing engine
user_stories.raw_text: We need to implement refund functionality in Billing. This will have impact in billing core to create credit and debit note, Integrations with 5 external systems out of which 3 are payment channels, 1 is upstream application from where the request is coming and last one is to send the refund information to ERP

user_stories.goal: I want to generate a debit note to handle refund reversals or adjustments
user_stories.benefit: so that we can correct financial discrepancies and maintain accurate ledger records
user_stories.raw_text: We need to implement refund functionality in Billing. This will have impact in billing core to create credit and debit note, Integrations with 5 external systems out of which 3 are payment channels, 1 is upstream application from where the request is coming and last one is to send the refund information to ERP

user_stories.goal: I want to route and process the refund transaction through the three external payment channels
user_stories.benefit: so that the funds are successfully returned to the customer's original payment method
user_stories.raw_text: We need to implement refund functionality in Billing. This will have impact in billing core to create credit and debit note, Integrations with 5 external systems out of which 3 are payment channels, 1 is upstream application from where the request is coming and last one is to send the refund information to ERP

user_stories.goal: I want the billing system to automatically transmit refund, credit note, and debit note details to the ERP system
user_stories.benefit: so that our general ledger and corporate financial reporting remain synchronized and compliant
user_stories.raw_text: We need to implement refund functionality in Billing. This will have impact in billing core to create credit and debit note, Integrations with 5 external systems out of which 3 are payment channels, 1 is upstream application from where the request is coming and last one is to send the refund information to ERP

classification.reasoning: The refund request is an External Input (EI) that processes data from an upstream system to trigger a refund. It references 2 FTRs (Transaction and Refund tables) and contains approximately 7 DETs.
Identified 4 data movements (1 Entry, 1 Read, 1 Write, 1 Exit) representing a standard transactional process.
No UI complexity as it is a pure API. Moderate integration risk due to security requirements, and moderate business logic for validating the refund request.
The process is classified as an External Input (EI) because it receives and processes an external refund request to update internal logical files (Customer Account and Credit Note).
5 data movements identified: 1 Entry, 1 Read, 2 Writes, and 1 Exit.
Backend-heavy process with low UI complexity but high business logic complexity due to financial validation and core billing integration.

Generating a debit note is classified as an External Output (EO) because it involves mathematical calculations for adjustments/reversals and updates the ledger. It references 3 FTRs (Refund, Ledger, Debit Note) and approximately 12 DETs.
The process consists of 1 Entry, 2 Reads, 1 Write, and 1 Exit, totaling 5 CFP.
While the UI is straightforward, the integration risk and business logic are high due to financial ledger consistency requirements and reversal calculations.
The process acts as an External Output (EO) that routes refund data to external payment channels, requiring 3 FTRs (Refund ILF, Gateway EIF, Account ILF) and 8 DETs.

there are 3 payment channels ie; Point of sale, online payment having visa, mastercard and Mada, and SADAD.  Also AI has flagged it.
This is an EI as well if there are adjustments that need to be made then this will be received as an Input

cosmic_movements.name: Receive Refund Request
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the refund request payload from the upstream system.

cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads the original transaction data to validate refund eligibility.

cosmic_movements.reasoning: Writes the new refund request record to the database.

cosmic_movements.reasoning: Sends the confirmation or error response back to the upstream system.

cosmic_movements.name: Receive Refund Request
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the refund request data from the triggering system.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Customer Account
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads customer account details to validate the refund request.

cosmic_movements.name: Receive Refund Request
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the trigger and details to initiate the refund.

cosmic_movements.reasoning: Sends the formatted financial details to the external ERP system API.

cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the success or error status back from the ERP system.

cosmic_movements.name: Update Sync Status
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Writes the synchronization status and ERP reference ID back to the local database.

cosmic_movements.reasoning: Outputs the confirmation or status of the credit note generation.

cosmic_movements.name: Receive Refund Request
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the trigger and details to initiate the refund.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Original Transaction
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads the original payment details to identify the correct channel.

cosmic_movements.name: Trigger Generation Request
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the trigger and parameters to generate the debit note.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Original Refund/Transaction
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads the original transaction details to calculate adjustments.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Ledger Account Details
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads account rules and current balances.

cosmic_movements.name: Write Debit Note Record
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Persists the newly created debit note record in the database.

cosmic_movements.name: Update Refund Status
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Writes the final transaction status to the internal database.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Ledger Account
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads current ledger account states to prepare the adjustment entry.

cosmic_movements.name: Write Debit Note
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Persists the newly created debit note record in the database.

cosmic_movements.name: Receive Refund Request
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the refund trigger and transaction details from the billing system.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Original Transaction
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads original payment details to identify the correct routing channel.

cosmic_movements.name: Send External Refund Request
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Sends the refund command to the selected external payment channel.

cosmic_movements.name: Receive External Confirmation
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the success or failure response from the external channel.

cosmic_movements.name: Update Refund Status
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Writes the final transaction status to the internal database.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Billing Transactions
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads the refund, credit note, or debit note details from the local database.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Integration Mapping
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads the GL account mapping and ERP endpoint configurations.

cosmic_movements.name: Send Gateway Refund Request
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Sends the refund transaction details to the selected external payment channel.

cosmic_movements.name: Receive Gateway Response
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the success or failure confirmation from the external payment channel.

cosmic_movements.name: Update Transaction Status
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Writes the updated refund status and reference ID to the internal database.

cosmic_movements.name: Confirm Refund Status
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Outputs the final processing status back to the billing system or user interface.

cosmic_movements.name: Send Refund to Gateway
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Sends the refund transaction to the selected external payment channel.

cosmic_movements.name: Receive Gateway Response
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the success or failure status from the external channel.

cosmic_movements.name: Present/Send Debit Note
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Outputs the generated debit note details to the user or external billing system.

cosmic_movements.name: Update Account Balance
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Updates the customer's account balance in the database.

cosmic_movements.name: Receive Debit Note Request
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Receives the trigger and details (original transaction ID, adjustment amount, reason) to initiate generation.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Original Transaction
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads the original transaction or refund details to validate the reversal.

cosmic_movements.name: Write Ledger Entry
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Updates or writes the adjustment entry to the ledger to maintain accurate records.

cosmic_movements.name: Present Debit Note
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Outputs the generated debit note details or confirmation back to the user/system.

cosmic_movements.name: Read Original Transaction
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Reads the original payment record to identify the correct payment method and channel.

cosmic_movements.name: Write Credit Note
cosmic_movements.reasoning: Creates and stores the new credit note record.
